shortDescription: Readiness checks for outpatient GI referrals driven by symptom notes, labs, stool testing, and medication context.
defaultReferralQuestion: Prepare a gastroenterology referral packet for persistent abdominal symptoms with alarm features.

requiredEvidence.label: Clear GI referral reason
requiredEvidence.description: The chart should explain the GI symptom cluster and why specialist input is needed.
requiredEvidence.rationale: GI triage is faster when the note clearly states the referral question.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Add a concise symptom-focused referral question with duration and alarm features.

requiredEvidence.label: Recent CBC data
requiredEvidence.description: Recent CBC or hemoglobin data should be available.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Anemia or blood loss concerns materially change GI urgency.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Order or attach a recent CBC before sending the referral.

requiredEvidence.label: Structured symptom history
requiredEvidence.description: Duration, triggers, and associated symptoms should be documented.
requiredEvidence.rationale: GI specialists need a coherent symptom narrative to triage correctly.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Document duration, weight change, bleeding, and prior treatment response.

requiredEvidence.label: Baseline chemistry or liver markers
requiredEvidence.description: Recent chemistry or liver-related labs should be visible.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Baseline labs help specialists prioritize workup and urgency.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Attach CMP or liver tests before sending the referral.

requiredEvidence.label: Active medication list
requiredEvidence.description: Current GI-relevant medications should be available.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Medication context changes GI differential and pre-visit planning.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Reconcile acid suppression, NSAID, and anticoagulant medications before referral.

optionalEvidence.label: Stool testing or occult blood data
optionalEvidence.description: Recent stool testing strengthens GI triage.
optionalEvidence.rationale: Helpful for triage but not mandatory in every case.
optionalEvidence.suggestedAction: Attach stool study results when available.

redFlags.label: Possible GI bleeding
redFlags.message: Possible bleeding or high-risk alarm finding may need expedited GI escalation.

redFlags.label: Unintentional weight loss
redFlags.message: Alarm symptoms suggest the referral should not be treated as routine.

packetSections.title: Referral Reason
packetSections.promptHint: Summarize the symptom pattern, timeline, and clinical question for GI.

packetSections.title: Pertinent GI History
packetSections.promptHint: Summarize alarm features, prior therapy, and symptom progression.

packetSections.title: Completed Workup
packetSections.promptHint: List available labs, stool studies, and related chart findings.

packetSections.title: Open Gaps Before Visit
packetSections.promptHint: Call out missing data that should be gathered before specialist review.
